topic: Two people discover they've been working on identical projects
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias stared at the blueprint spread across the drafting table, the faint smell of stale coffee suddenly overwhelming. He blinked, rubbing his eyes hard, expecting the fluorescent lights to flicker, but everything remained stubbornly, perfectly normal. Then he looked across the room, and there, seated at a neighboring desk, was another person—a stranger—also meticulously tracing the same complex circuit diagram. A cold knot tightened in Elias’s stomach. He slowly pushed his chair back, the scrape of metal on linoleum sounding deafeningly loud in the quiet office. He wanted to vanish. He could feel a flush creeping up his neck, hot and undeniable. He snatched his notebook, his hand trembling slightly, and fled toward the exit, desperate to escape the suffocating symmetry of the situation.

[story 2]

“This is impossible,” Sarah whispered, her voice thin and reedy as she surveyed the data logs on her screen. She leaned closer, her breath catching in her throat as she recognized the proprietary algorithm running on her machine—an algorithm she had spent three sleepless weeks coding. Across the aisle, Mark was leaning back, a slow, unnerving smile spreading across his face as he reviewed the exact same lines of code. Sarah felt a sudden, intense wave of heat bloom across her cheeks. She gripped the edge of her desk so tightly her knuckles turned white. Every muscle in her body seemed to seize up. She cleared her throat, a dry, rasping sound, and began methodically gathering her peripherals, her movements jerky and overly precise, as if trying to erase the evidence of her shared endeavor.

[story 3]

"Wait a minute," Liam mumbled, dropping his stylus onto the schematic. He looked up, and there, across the open workspace, was Chloe, her face etched with the same furrow of concentration. They were both wrestling with the final iteration of the architectural design—the load-bearing calculations were identical. A profound sense of awkwardness washed over Liam, making him want to sink through the floor. He shifted his weight from one foot to the other, a nervous habit he couldn't shake. He cleared his throat repeatedly, the sound sounding embarrassingly loud in the cavernous studio. He quickly shuffled toward the supply cabinet, needing the solid, impersonal presence of something tangible to anchor him against the dizzying realization that his entire creative process had been mirrored by a complete duplicate.
